name: Wide Crime Scene
description: Figures scattered across a space thick with evidence, the environment telling its own story.
hld_patterns: {setting}. The space speaks louder than anything its occupants could say.
{setting}. Figures dot the landscape like punctuation in a sentence no one has finished reading.
{setting}. Every object here is a question; the answers are still being gathered.

1: {subject}, center of frame, facing away from camera, small in frame.
2: {subject}, left third of frame, facing right, small in frame.
{subject}, right third of frame, facing left, small in frame, slightly further from lens.
3: {subject}, far left of frame, facing right, foreground, mid-scale.
{subject}, center of frame, facing away, furthest from lens, smallest in frame.
{subject}, far right of frame, facing left, mid-depth.
4: {subject}, far left of frame, facing right, foreground.
{subject}, far right of frame, facing away from camera, furthest from lens.

background_pool: Vast abandoned warehouse, shafts of daylight cutting through broken skylights, debris scattered across concrete floors in patterns that feel deliberate
Snow-covered field at dusk, a trail of disturbances leading toward a dark tree line, the sky bruised purple and grey
Apartment interior frozen mid-disarray, drawers pulled out, contents strewn across the floor, a single lamp still burning warm against the cold palette
Underground parking garage, fluorescent lights buzzing and flickering, water pooling on the oil-stained concrete, support pillars creating rhythmic shadows into the distance
action_pool: Standing motionless, arms at sides, head down
crouching low, one knee lowered, torso bent forward
One arm raised, hand shielding the eyes
Torso turned fully to one side, weight on the back foot
Both hands in pockets, shoulders raised high
Walking slowly, one foot placed deliberately ahead of the other
Arms hanging loose, body completely still
One hand raised to the chin, elbow supported by the other arm
Back to the camera, shoulders slack, head turned slightly to one side
